tldr: Freee is a JP-only accounting + invoicing platform; Fonoa is a global VAT/GST and e-invoicing API — migrate when the business expands beyond Japan and needs multi-country tax automation.
why_switch: Selling into 40+ countries and need global VAT/GST coverage
Real-time tax calc and e-invoicing across jurisdictions
API-first architecture that fits a modern SaaS backend
Decoupling tax from JP accounting so the ledger can stay in Freee or move
Need invoice validation and clearance flows beyond JP qualified invoice
what_to_export: Customer master with country and VAT/tax IDs
Historical invoices and tax line items
JP qualified invoice numbers and metadata
Tax rates and rules currently configured
API integration points calling Freee for tax
export_method: Use Freee's REST API and admin exports to pull customers, invoices, and product data (CSV / API). Snapshot any JP-specific tax logic and qualified-invoice metadata before cutting over. Consult Freee's official developer documentation for current export endpoints rather than assuming names.
import_method: Fonoa is a tax automation API, not a bookkeeping system, so you don't import invoices wholesale — you integrate Fonoa at the point of tax determination and e-invoice issuance. Build a thin adapter that calls Fonoa for calc/validation/clearance per transaction and decide whether your ledger continues in Freee or moves elsewhere. Verify endpoint shapes against Fonoa's official documentation.

concept_mapping.note: Fonoa handles tax/e-invoice; ledger stays in your accounting system.
JP becomes one country among many; rules differ per jurisdiction.
Map JP-specific fields into Fonoa's per-country schema.
Pass country + tax ID per call; Fonoa is largely stateless on customers.
Different auth, endpoints, and rate limits.
Filing/remittance scope varies — confirm what Fonoa covers per country.

gotchas: Fonoa replaces tax automation, not accounting — keep a ledger somewhere
JP qualified invoice rules don't translate 1:1 to other jurisdictions
Check exact country coverage and what Fonoa files vs reports
Run both systems in parallel for one full close before cutover
Rate limits and pricing should be validated for your transaction volume
Confirm all features and endpoints against Fonoa's official documentation

email.subject: Moving tax stack from Freee to Fonoa for global expansion
email.body:
Hi team,

As we expand outside Japan, Freee's JP-only tax model is becoming a constraint. The plan is to keep accounting where it is for now and route tax determination and e-invoicing through Fonoa, which covers VAT/GST and e-invoice flows in 40+ countries.

We'll export customers, invoices, and tax mappings from Freee, then build an adapter that calls Fonoa per transaction for calc, validation, and clearance. JP-specific qualified-invoice rules will be remapped into Fonoa's per-country schema.

Estimated effort is 3-8 weeks with one full close in parallel before cutover. I'll confirm country coverage and pricing with Fonoa before we lock the plan.

Thanks
